fr:blockquote.fr:p: I am interested in developing logical and algebraic approaches for understanding the structure of computation. Most of my work is centred around the semantic modelling of programming languages. In this endeavour I find that type theory and category theory are particularly useful in uncovering the structure of programs, circumscribing their possible behaviours, and relating them to various mathematical models.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Syllepsis in homotopy type theory
fr:frontmatter.fr:meta: LICS ’22: Proceedings of the 37th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science

@inproceedings{sojakova-kavvos-2022, author = {Sojakova, Kristina and Kavvos, G. A.}, title = {Syllepsis in Homotopy Type Theory}, year = {2022}, isbn = {9781450393515}, publisher = {Association for Computing Machinery}, address = {New York, NY, USA}, doi = {10.1145/3531130.3533347}, booktitle = {Proceedings of the 37th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science}, articleno = {29}, numpages = {12}, location = {Haifa, Israel}, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The Eckmann–Hilton argument shows that any two monoid structures on the same set satisfying the interchange law are in fact the same operation, which is moreover commutative. When the monoids correspond to the vertical and horizontal composition of a sufficiently higher-dimensional category, the Eckmann–Hilton argument itself appears as a higher cell. This cell is often required to satisfy an additional piece of coherence, which is known as the syllepsis. We show that the syllepsis can be constructed from the elimination rule of intensional identity types in Martin-Löf type theory.
